steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Label the side pair for the named angle first: opposite, adjacent, and hypotenuse. Then pick the matching shortcut, such as SOH/CAH/TOA, complementary trig values, or a special right-triangle ratio.

steps.title: Identify the right angle and triangle structure
steps.content: Since angles $D$ and $E$ are acute in right triangle $DEF$, the right angle must be at $F$. This makes $DF$ and $EF$ the two legs of the triangle.

steps.title: Set up the tangent ratio for angle E
steps.content:
For angle $E$ in a right triangle:
$$\tan E = \frac{\text{opposite}}{\text{adjacent}} = \frac{DF}{EF}$$
Substituting the known values $\tan E = \frac{1}{6}$ and $DF = 18.6$:
$$\frac{1}{6} = \frac{18.6}{EF}$$
